entry.title: SAP Signavio July 2026 Product Release - Process Transformation Management and Collaboration
entry.content:
SAP Signavio July 2026 Product Release - Process Transformation Management and Collaboration

I’m happy to share with you the SAP Signavio July product release for process transformation and collaboration. This release will enable you to get started faster with personalized, role-based onboarding powered by AI and be able to unlock measurable value from everywhere and move from insights to action faster.

We're looking forward to have the new features available to you on July 28, 2026! Let’s dive in!

 

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

Unlock measurable value from everywhere and move from insights to action faster

Transformation value doesn't exist in just one system and now your value management doesn't have to either. With our new Value Case API, SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager enables you to seamlessly integrate value cases from third-party and partner solutions into a single, connected view of transformation value across your technology landscape. Whether your value data originates from finance, ERP, reporting platforms, or partner applications, you can consolidate it in one place to create a holistic view of business impact and value realization. Key benefits include:

  • Create a single source of truth for transformation value by consolidating value cases from SAP Signavio and external solutions into one unified view.
  • Reduce manual effort and duplicate work by eliminating the need to maintain the same value cases across multiple disconnected tools.
  • Improve reporting and portfolio governance by making value data consistently available for downstream reporting, analytics, and executive decision-making.
  • Extend value management across your enterprise with secure APIs that allow value case data to be managed programmatically while respecting permissions and licensing.
  • Enable partner and customer integrations so implementation partners and IT teams can seamlessly embed value management into their own workflows and transformation toolchains.

The result is a connected transformation ecosystem where value data flows seamlessly across systems, helping organizations make better-informed decisions, accelerate value realization, and demonstrate measurable business outcomes.

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

Get started faster with personalized, role-based onboarding powered by AI

Finding the right process information shouldn't slow you down. With our new AI-assisted role-based process overview, users can get up and running faster by instantly accessing the process content most relevant to their role without navigating complex process hierarchies or manually configuring views. You can simply provide your job details, and our AI-powered onboarding wizard recommends the most relevant roles from your organization's role dictionary. After a quick review and selection, you'll immediately gain access to a personalized process overview tailored to your responsibilities, helping you find the information you need from day one. Key benefits include:

  • Accelerate time-to-productivity with AI-powered role recommendations that quickly connect users to the right process content.
  • Deliver a personalized experience by automatically surfacing the process information most relevant to each user's role-all in one place.
  • Eliminate manual searching and navigation so you can spend less time looking for information and more time getting work done.
  • Reduce administrative effort by minimizing manual configuration of role-specific views and access paths while ensuring you see the content that matters most.

This AI-powered onboarding experience helps organizations improve user adoption, simplify access to process knowledge, and enable you to become productive faster.

Want to know what else is coming out as part of this release?

Check out our highlight blog for SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite and find out what else we have prepared!

entry.title: Re: SF Compensation Budget Rule Configuration
entry.content:
I am making the assumption that by forwarding you mean routing the form to the next step in the workflow.   There is no current configuration option that would conditionally allow or prevent routing of forms.  

Forwarding of forms is not a leading practice as each form has data for multiple people and based on it being sensitive data, we recommend you only share data on compensation as needed.

entry.title: Demo Code and get the Title of SAP DEMO JAM 2026 winner!
entry.content:
Picture this: your team is building something incredibly cool with SAP Business AI Platform, maybe integrations, AI core, Joule for Consultants, BDC....   You're solving a real business problem, poking at new features, maybe leaning on a component using AI in a new way to move faster, and genuinely having fun while you do it.

 

Now picture doing that live, on stage, in front of an amazing crowd of SAP fans (friends, really) at SAP TechEd Berlin.

 

You've probably guessed it by now. Demo Jam is back, and we're counting on this community to bring the goods.
 
For the newcomers, here's the recap
 
Demo Jam is an interactive, competitive showcase. No decks, no fluff, just real demos and real personality on stage.

To participate, you submit an idea and then bring it to life live in front of your peers. **No slides.** Just a real demo and whatever makes your team, your team. Six teams will get the stage, six minutes each, and the crowd decides the winner with the trusty applause-o-meter. Make some noise.  Only 2 speakers per team will get Lecture-Only passes, but of course more colleagues can help you behind the scenes.
 
Before you submit, a few things to know
 
  • Timing is everything. You get 6 minutes, and only 6 minutes. Go over, and the buzzer finds you. Nobody likes a buzzkill.
  • The demo has to be live. No slides, no pictures, no videos, no AI-generated stand-ins. If it's not a real demo, it's not a jam.  Also, sales and marketing submissions won't make it past review, have code and live systems ready to show.
  • Show us how you used SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Business Data Cloud, and/or SAP Business AI to solve an actual business use case. We want to see the problem and the solution.
  • This year, Demo Jam takes the stage in the Community Clubhouse, on the Community Theater stage.
Let's talk details
1. **Who can participate?** All SAP customers, on their own or alongside an SAP partner, can submit a proposal, not partners only.
2. **How do I participate?** Submit your proposal with all the details **Demo Jam 2026 Submissions**.
3. **What happens after I submit?** Our SAP experts review every submission and select up to 6 teams for the Demo Jam final in Berlin.
4. **How many team members are allowed?** No limit on the team building the demo, but only 2 team members can present on stage.
 
Here's our timeline
 
We're moving fast this year, so mark your calendar now.

1. Submit your proposal between **July 23 and August 5**.
2. We'll review and select teams **August 6 through August 14**.
3. Selected teams will receive confirmation and next steps **after August 14**.
4. Finalize and rehearse your demo, submissions locked in by **September 28**.
5. Jam live on stage during SAP TechEd Berlin, will be end of day 1 or 2, TBD between **October 27-29**.
5. **Do we get SAP TechEd tickets?** Each selected team gets 2 lecture-only tickets (upgradeable to Full Conference, if seats remain, by paying the difference).
6. **Is travel and expense covered?** No, T&E is not covered.

Demo Jam timeline: submit July 23-August 5, review and select August 6-14, confirmation after August 14, finalize demo by September 28, jam live October 27-28
To submit your proposal, fill out the details **Demo Jam 2026 Submissions**. Looking forward to seeing what this community brings to the stage.  And if you aren't able to squeeze your topic into 6 minutes, then maybe read thei post to submit a session for our Community Theater 20 min talks here.

See you in Berlin. Let's jam!

entry.title: Will you be one of our SAP TechEd Community Theater Speakers in Berlin? Submit now!
entry.content:
Alright, SAP community. It's that time again.
Every year I get to stand up (beard freshly blued, thank you) and ask the same question: who's got something worth sharing?   And every year, you all prove that some of the best sessions at SAP TechEd don't come from a our teams internally, but they come from you, in the trenches, building real things with SAP tech and living to tell the tale.
 
So here we go again. Are you knee-deep in SAP BTP architecture?  Have you finally tamed ABAP Cloud extensibility and want to show the scars? Poking at Joule for Developers and found something nobody's talking about yet?  This is your shot to bring it to the Community Theater stage in Berlin.

 

How was that for a pitch?  If it landed, keep reading, here's everything you need to know.   Here is a view of a CT Talk overflowing last year with standing room only, maybe this could be you this year.
 
But first, the headline: **we need your Community Theater submission in the next two weeks by August 5th.** Don't let this one slip past you.
 
 
What you need to know
  • Community Talks are 20-minute sessions. Presentation, demo, improv, props, a game if that's your thing, whatever gets your point across.
  • This format is BY the community, FOR the community. Lessons learned, best practices, "here's the thing I built and here's what broke along the way," it's all fair game.
  • If your proposal is accepted, you'll be paired with one of our Developer Advocates or another community leader as your co-speaker to help introduce you and get your talk going.
  • Accepted speakers get one Lecture-Only ticket to SAP TechEd Berlin (upgradeable to Full Conference, if seats remain, by paying the difference).
  • We have approximately [30] Lecture-Only passes to award this year, so bring your best. (you can have multiple speakers in your session but only one pass awarded to the submitter of the CT Talk)
  • Travel and expenses are not covered by SAP.
I'm in! How do I participate?
Quick heads up again: we're running an accelerated timeline this year, so don't sit on this one. You've got **two weeks** to get your Community Theater submission in.
 
**In a nutshell:**
1. Submit your proposal between **July 23 and August 5**.
2. We'll review and select submissions **August 6 through August 14**.
3. Our goal is for selected speakers to receive invite instructions **after August 14**, including your Developer Advocate co-speaker pairing and speaker pass.  This date is subject to logistics approval.
4. Submit your session material (presentation, demo recording) for review by **September 28**.
5. Deliver your session live during SAP TechEd Berlin, on your assigned day between **October 27-29**.
 

 
Yes, it's a tighter window than usual. We're doing it because we want to get selections and speaker passes into your hands as early as possible and ensure you can choose from our new product tracks to align with your speech topic.   We'll coordinate scheduling as best we can so you can deliver your session and still catch the other sessions you want to see.  And maybe watch or participate in this year's DemoJam!!! 

To submit your proposal, fill out the details ** Community Theater Talk Submission Form**.  The more detail you give us, the easier it is for us to picture your session on that stage, so don't hold back. 

See you in Berlin. Let's see what this community brings to the stage!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Topics
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung 

I would love to host a CodeJam at the Montreal location. The "Real-time data for AI agents: Grounding with event-driven architecture" seems to be perfect that enables hands on learning as well and combines real world use cases of SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh, and vector grounding effectively

If in case Joule is preferable as a strong topic, let me know if the following topics would be more preferable than the above

  • Joule Studio: A hands-on session covering how to build, test, and deploy custom Joule extensions. Attendees could walk through creating skills, configuring orchestration flows, and connecting to SAP BTP services.

  • Joule for Developers: Focused on the developer experience: using Joule as a coding assistant within SAP Build Code, leveraging it for CAP application development, generating unit tests, and understanding how to provide effective context and prompts for SAP-specific development tasks.

  • Building AI-powered integrations with Joule and SAP AI Core: Combining Joule's orchestration capabilities with custom AI models deployed on SAP AI Core, covering the end-to-end pattern from prompt engineering through to grounding with enterprise data.

We have a venue that can accommodate 20–30 participants and a team ready to help with logistics. Looking forward to hearing about next steps!

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA AI at Code:Connect 2026-07 Recap
entry.content:
During Code:Connect 2026, we held an SAP CodeJam event in St. Leon-Rot, Germany. The event focused on exploring the foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud: Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine.

Thanks to all participants who joined, and also to @ChristophMorgen for his presentation!

entry.title: mass data management
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am working on the following SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Position Management exercise:
Create a Mass Data Management request to change the Location to Denver (US_DEN) for all positions starting with MGR_ within the ACE_USA Legal Entity and ACE_IND Business Unit. Assign a Matrix Manager using the incumbent of the new position created in the previous task. Verify that synchronization of Job Relationships from the position hierarchy is enabled, then run the mass change.
I have completed the Mass Data Management request successfully, and the Location is updated to Denver for all relevant positions.
However, the Matrix Relationship is not updating after the mass change.
I have already verified:
Position hierarchy
Position Management settings
Job Relationship synchronization settings
Is there any additional configuration or prerequisite that I should check for Matrix Relationship synchronization during Mass Data Management?
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: EWM Pick Point for Full and Partial Pallet
entry.content:
Hi EWM Experts,
We are looking to implement a Pick Point process for Production Staging (PP-EWM Integration).
Current Process:

Warehouse Tasks are generated via /SCWM/STAGE from PMRs.
Full pallet stock is stored in a dedicated HU-managed storage type.
Partial pallets are stored in a separate storage type.


Has anybody adapted this design for Production Staging using PMRs and /SCWM/STAGE?
We are particularly interested in understanding how LOSC, POSC, Storage Process determination, and Warehouse Process Types behave for staging-generated WTs.
Any guidance or real project experience would be very helpful.

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - Are you looking for official blog posts? Blog INDEX
entry.content:
Dear @antoniojmnunes ,

Do you know if is possible to create multiple databases inside the same PostgreSQL instance on SAP BTP?, if the answer is yes, do you know how we can setup an SAP CAP services to use a specific database name?

My idea is having multiple databases inside the same PostgreSQL instance on SAP BTP and then be able to isolate schemas among multiple SAP CAP services.

Kind regards

entry.title: Move SB 2SL to CBC 3SL Asks
entry.content:
1. Mixed purchase invoice docking:
Currently, the standard 2SL interface cannot place Goods Issue and Goods Return on the same invoice. Can 3SL be solved by custom development or other solutions? Hope SAP expert to share a solution or the logic/ideas.
混合采购发票对接：
目前 2SL的标准接口无法将“发货”和“退货”放在同一张发票上。请问 3SL能否通过自定义开发或其他方案解决？希望分享方案或实现思路。

2. Interface creation process:  
What is the difference for 3SL vs 2SL regarding the creation process of custom and standard interfaces? We hope that the interface development process in 3SL can be demonstrated via a video sharing.
接口创建流程：
自定义接口和标准接口的创建流程 3SLvs 2SL有什么不同？希望有演示视频分享。

3. Interface Extensibility Benefits:
Where exactly is stronger for 3SL vs 2SL in API interface expansion? We would like to give you an example of what 2SL can’t do.
接口拓展性优势：
在API接口拓展性上3SL vs 2SL具体强在哪里？希望能举例说明它可以实现哪些 2SL做不到的功能。


4. Migration of nearly 100 existing interfaces:
We are currently using nearly 100 custom and standard interfaces (attached) for 2SL, can these old interfaces be smooth compatible after upgrading to 3 SL, or do we need to rewrite them all?
现有近 100 个接口的迁移：
我们目前在用的自定义和标准接口将近 100 个（参见附件），2SL升级 3SL后这些旧接口能平滑兼容吗，还是需要全部重构重写？

5. Operations vs. UX Differences: What is the clear difference for 3SL vs 2SL regarding IT daily operations management and the business user interface, usage habits?
运维与用户体验差异：在 IT 日常运维管理以及业务用户的操作界面、使用习惯上，3SL vs2 SL有哪些明显的区别？

entry.title: How to match Intercompany Stock Transfer , COGS & Revenue posting in Intercompany Matching ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a question regarding Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation (ICMR) for intercompany stock transfer scenarios.

For stock transfer transactions, the sending company posts:

  • COGS at the time of outbound delivery.

  • Intercompany Revenue when the corresponding intercompany billing document is created.

Since both postings reside in the sending company (leading unit), how should these P&L postings be handled in ICMR? Is it recommended to:

  • Configure a dedicated matching method for stock transfers (e.g., based on GL accounts and Billing Type = CBST), or

  • Use another standard approach for matching these documents?

Additionally, we have a standard intercompany sales scenario where:

  • IC Revenue and IC Receivable from the intercompany billing document in the sending company are matched with

  • IC COGS (599*) and IC Payable from the supplier invoice in the receiving company.

Would it be a good practice to combine both intercompany sales and intercompany stock transfer P&L reconciliation into a single matching method, or should they be maintained separately?

I would appreciate any recommendations or best practices from those who have implemented ICMR for these scenarios.

Thanks,
Sagar

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Planning Date Dimensions change request
entry.content: Try that—it should work. Set Date as a column. Then click the three dots next to it, select Hierarchy, and choose Flat presentation. After that, right-click the column and select Show totals. I think you can also choose whether the total appears on the right or left.

entry.title: AI Adoption & Activation – Getting the Most from SuccessFactors AI - Session Three

AI Adoption & Activation – Getting the Most from SuccessFactors AI

 

Join us for an interactive discussion bringing together SuccessFactors customers and  SAP AI subject matter and transformation experts to explore what truly drives AI success in 2026 — moving beyond simple activation.

 

Session Three

Scaling, Operating Model, Governance, and the Future of AI in HR

In this session, we’ll cover:

  • Practical examples of what has worked across organisations

This session is designed to help you turn strategy into action—driving meaningful behaviour change and realising measurable business value from your initiatives.

entry.title: Integrating Amazon FBA with SAP S4 HANA - Part 3
entry.content:
Just in case you landed here directly, this is the third chapter in this series about integrating SAP S/4HANA with Amazon Seller Central. This article focuses on Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA integration.

Unlike FBM, Amazon FBA is best modelled as a consignment process because Amazon physically holds and ships the inventory while the merchant continues to own it until the product is sold.

  • In the first part I covered Amazon merchant models, the Selling Partner API, master data setup, and the process of obtaining an Amazon Developer ID. Here is the link to the article How to Get Started with SAP S/4HANA and Amazon Seller Central Integration 
  • In the second part on Fulfilled by Merchant, I explained how Amazon FBM maps to the standard SAP Sales and Distribution order-to-cash cycle.

That distinction changes how inventory, revenue, receivables, tax, returns, reimbursements, and Amazon settlements should be recorded in SAP S/4HANA.

 

US division of a European Food Processing Company closed down their Amazon marketplace simply because they weren't able to manage the processes and reconcile their financials.  
A nutrition supplement company outsources their complete Amazon operations for a significant fees to a third party provider.

If you Amazon FBA is the model you are exploring for your business, this tutorial will help simplify the entire concept and give you some confidence 😊 of mapping the process in the SAP terminologies. 

Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA Integration: Complete Consignment Process

Amazon Fulfilled by Amazon, commonly called FBA, allows merchants to send inventory in bulk to Amazon fulfillment centers. Amazon then stores the inventory and manages order picking, packing, customer delivery, customer service, and returns.

In SAP S/4HANA, this model closely matches the customer consignment process. The goods remain the merchant’s property while they are stored at Amazon. Revenue is recognized only when a customer purchases the product and Amazon fulfills the order.

Amazon FBA and SAP S/4HANA: Key Takeaways

Here are the key takeaways from mapping Amazon FBA with Consignment Process within SAP. 

  • Stock sent to Amazon is recorded as customer consignment stock.
  • Inventory remains owned by the merchant until the customer sale.
  • No revenue is recognized when goods are initially sent to Amazon.
  • A customer purchase creates a consignment issue in SAP.
  • Customer returns create consignment returns and credit adjustments.
  • Unsold stock returned by Amazon creates a consignment pick-up.
  • Amazon settlements must be matched against sales, fees, returns, reserves, and reimbursements.
  • Special stock indicator W keeps Amazon-held inventory separate and visible.

What Is Amazon FBA? What are the steps involved business process wise

Business process wise Amazon FBA cycle starts with the following steps.

  • Identifying merchandize you will be selling - A big decision is which of your products you would want to launch on Amazon FBA store. Of course these are subsets of products that have a B2C demand and some market research to back it up. 
  • Sending a bulk shipment to Amazon Warehouse / Distribution Center - The first shipment that you sent to Amazon Warehouse, it often a butterfly in the stomach instance. You need to create a shipment prior to this with all the items that you intend to send to Amazon. You may also do some carrier shopping. In many cases I have seen Amazon's own carrier being the most cost effective.

 

  • When Amazon order comes through - As customers place orders, Amazon fulfills them and sends the information back to SAP. Each order is recorded as a consignment issue. In SAP, Amazon remains both the sold-to party and the payer, while the ship-to address is the end customer. Having the correct ship-to address is critical because it determines the tax calculation. The tax shown in SAP should match what Amazon collects and pays on behalf of the seller. Since pricing is driven by Amazon, you will need manual conditions within SAP to sync the pricing components to match Amazon will pay you at the period end. 

When Amazon Order comes to SAP, you need to ensure that you map correct ASIN number into customer material Number and the Net Line item and Order amount should match what Amazon will pay you in their settlement cycle for this order. 

Having synchronization at periodic intervals, keeps your A/R accurate. 

  • Returns are also tracked. When Amazon processes a return, SAP creates a consignment return, followed by a return delivery and credit memo. This keeps your accounts receivable aligned with Amazon’s adjustments.
  • Finally, Payment reconciliation takes place. Every two weeks, Amazon sends a bulk payment that covers all orders, less fees, reserves, or special charges. With proper integration, the invoices in SAP match Amazon’s payouts, reducing manual work. Although there may still be small adjustments for account-level charges, most of the process runs automatically, saving time and effort for the finance team.

Under Amazon FBA, merchants do not ship each customer order themselves. Instead, they create an inbound shipment and send inventory in bulk to Amazon’s fulfillment network.

Amazon then handles inventory storage, order picking, packaging, delivery to the customer, customer service, customer returns, as well as selected inventory adjustments and reimbursements. This allows sellers to outsource most fulfillment operations while Amazon manages the end-to-end logistics and post-purchase experience.

The merchant continues to manage product listings, pricing, procurement, accounting, and inventory ownership.

Why Merchants Choose Amazon FBA

Merchants choose Amazon FBA because it reduces logistics work and gives customers access to Amazon’s fulfillment network.

1. Lower Logistics Overhead

Merchants no longer need to operate their own pick-pack-ship process for FBA products. This can reduce warehouse workload, packaging effort, and carrier management.

2. Faster Customer Delivery

Amazon distributes inventory across its fulfillment network and places products closer to buyers. This can shorten delivery estimates and improve the customer experience.

3. Prime Eligibility and Buyer Trust

FBA products may qualify for Prime delivery. Customers also receive Amazon-managed tracking, support, and returns, which can increase confidence in the purchase.

4. Lower Exposure to Carrier Delays

In an FBM model, the merchant is responsible for carrier selection and delivery performance. Late deliveries can affect the merchant’s Amazon performance metrics.

Under FBA, Amazon assumes much of this fulfillment responsibility.

5. Greater Sales Potential

Fast delivery, Prime eligibility, and Amazon-backed customer service can make an FBA listing more attractive than a comparable merchant-fulfilled listing.

The trade-off is that Amazon gains physical control of the inventory while the merchant continues to own it. This is the main reason FBA should be treated as a consignment process in SAP.

Why Amazon FBA Is a Consignment Process in SAP

When products are stored inside an Amazon fulfillment center, they remain the merchant’s inventory.

Amazon has physical custody of the goods, but ownership has not transferred. No customer sale has occurred, and revenue should not be recognised.

This is the standard definition of consignment stock: goods stored at a customer or partner location that remain the seller’s property until they are consumed or sold.

SAP S/4HANA provides a four-step consignment cycle that maps closely to Amazon FBA.

What Is Special Stock Indicator W?

Special stock indicator W identifies customer consignment stock in SAP.

It allows Amazon-held inventory to remain visible and valued as an asset while keeping it separate from normal unrestricted stock in the merchant’s warehouse.

This distinction is important for inventory reporting, balance-sheet accuracy, available-to-promise calculations, and reconciliation with Amazon’s inventory reports.

Amazon FBA Integration Architecture

A complete Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA integration normally connects Amazon Seller Central, Amazon Selling Partner API, SAP Sales and Distribution, Materials Management, Finance, and an integration platform or middleware layer.

The integration must support bidirectional data movement.

Data Moving from SAP to Amazon

  • Product and SKU data
  • Inventory planned for inbound shipments
  • Shipment and carton details
  • Product identifiers and mappings
  • Selected pricing or availability information

Data Moving from Amazon to SAP

  • FBA customer orders
  • Shipment and fulfillment data
  • Customer delivery addresses
  • Returns and refunds
  • FBA inventory balances
  • Lost and damaged inventory adjustments
  • Amazon fees and commissions
  • Reimbursements
  • Settlement and payout reports

Master Data Required for Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA Integration

Reliable master data is the foundation of the integration. Incorrect mappings will eventually create inventory differences, tax errors, unmatched settlements, and incorrect receivables.

Amazon as Customer or Business Partner

Amazon must be created as a customer or business partner because the SAP consignment cycle is executed against a customer account.

Figure - Create Amazon as a business partner against which the consignment cycle will execute. 

In a common FBA design:

  • Sold-To Party: Amazon
  • Payer: Amazon
  • Ship-To: The end customer’s delivery address

Amazon is responsible for settling the merchant’s proceeds, but the end customer’s address is required for tax calculation and transaction reporting.

Amazon Consignment Inventory Structure

The merchant must decide how Amazon-held inventory will be represented in SAP.

Depending on the implementation, FBA stock may be organised through a dedicated plant, storage location, customer consignment structure, or reporting dimension.

Many businesses maintain a dedicated storage location or reporting structure so Amazon inventory is never confused with stock physically available in their own warehouse.

ASIN, Seller SKU, and SAP Material Mapping

Every Amazon ASIN or Seller SKU must map to the correct SAP material number.

This mapping may be maintained through Customer Material Information Records or an integration-layer mapping table.

The mapping should work in both directions:

  • Amazon ASIN or Seller SKU to SAP material number
  • SAP material number to Amazon ASIN or Seller SKU

This mapping is required for fill-ups, customer sales, returns, inventory adjustments, reimbursements, and settlement reconciliation.

Amazon Fee and SAP Condition-Type Mapping

Amazon commissions, FBA fulfillment fees, discounts, promotions, and other order-level charges should be mapped to appropriate SAP pricing condition types.

This allows the SAP net receivable to align with the order-level amount Amazon reports as payable.

 

Consignment Fill-Up: Sending Inventory to Amazon

The FBA process begins when the merchant sends inventory to Amazon.

This step involves coordinated activity in Amazon Seller Central and SAP S/4HANA.

Amazon-Side Inbound Shipment Process

In Seller Central, the merchant creates an inbound shipment or FBA shipment plan.

The merchant provides:

  • Products being sent
  • Quantity of each product
  • Source warehouse
  • Carton configuration
  • Preparation details
  • Shipping method

Amazon provides item labels, carton labels, preparation instructions, and fulfillment-center destinations.

Amazon may divide one inbound shipment across several fulfillment centers based on its inventory-placement requirements.

SAP Consignment Fill-Up Process

In SAP S/4HANA, the corresponding process normally includes:

  1. Creation of a consignment fill-up sales order
  2. Creation of an outbound delivery
  3. Picking and packing
  4. Post Goods Issue

The goods issue moves stock from the merchant’s warehouse into Amazon customer consignment stock under special stock indicator W.

No Revenue at Consignment Fill-Up

No revenue or customer receivable should be posted when inventory is sent to Amazon.

The merchant has moved inventory to another physical location, but the inventory has not yet been sold.

Recognising revenue during the fill-up would overstate sales, receivables, profit, and potentially tax.

Tracking Multiple Amazon Fulfillment Centers

Amazon may send an inbound shipment to several fulfillment centers and later redistribute units within its network.

SAP does not always need to represent every fulfillment center as a separate accounting location. Many merchants track the aggregate consignment quantity held by Amazon while allowing Amazon to manage fulfillment-center-level placement.

Businesses requiring detailed operational reporting can maintain additional fulfillment-center visibility in the integration layer.

Consignment Issue: Processing Amazon FBA Customer Orders

When a customer buys a product, Amazon picks, packs, and ships it from an FBA fulfillment center.

Amazon then makes the order and fulfillment information available through SP-API endpoints and reports.

In SAP, the completed customer sale is recorded as a consignment issue.

This is the moment when:

  • The product leaves special stock W.
  • Ownership transfers to the buyer.
  • Revenue is recognised.
  • The Amazon receivable is posted.
  • Relevant fees and taxes are recorded.

Amazon Order Number

The Amazon order number should be stored in a searchable SAP reference field. Many implementations map it to the customer purchase-order field.

This reference is required for customer service, returns, settlement matching, and audit trails.

Sold-To, Payer, and Ship-To Determination

Amazon normally remains the Sold-To Party and Payer because Amazon settles the merchant’s proceeds.

The end customer’s address is used as the Ship-To information. This address is especially important for tax determination.

Product and Quantity Mapping

Each Amazon SKU or ASIN must resolve to the correct SAP material. The consignment issue must consume the correct quantity from Amazon consignment stock.

Amazon Fees and Net Receivables

Amazon commissions, FBA fulfillment fees, discounts, and other order-level charges should be represented through SAP pricing condition types or related financial postings.

A useful validation rule is:

If the SAP net receivable does not match Amazon’s order-level payable amount, the fee or pricing mapping is incomplete.

Amazon Marketplace Facilitator Tax in SAP

Tax requires special attention in an Amazon FBA integration.

Under Marketplace Facilitator laws in the United States and similar rules in other regions, Amazon may calculate, collect, and remit sales tax on behalf of the merchant.

Why the Ship-To Address Matters

Tax generally follows the customer’s delivery destination. The end customer’s address must therefore be available in SAP even when Amazon is the Sold-To Party and Payer.

The ship-to address allows SAP to determine the correct tax jurisdiction and compare the result with Amazon’s tax reporting.

Statistical Tax Condition

Amazon-collected tax is often represented as a statistical condition in SAP.

This keeps the amount visible and reconcilable without incorrectly treating it as tax the merchant must remit where Amazon is responsible for remittance.

The SAP tax amount should align with Amazon’s Marketplace Facilitator reporting. A close match indicates that the ship-to and tax determination logic is working correctly.

Consignment Return: Processing Amazon Customer Returns

When Amazon accepts a customer return and places the product back into sellable FBA inventory, the transaction should create a consignment return in SAP.

The SAP process normally includes:

  1. Consignment return order
  2. Return delivery
  3. Inventory movement back into special stock W
  4. Credit memo linked to the original transaction

The return should reference the original Amazon order and the original SAP consignment issue whenever possible.

This ensures that SAP:

  • Reduces or reverses the original receivable
  • Restores eligible inventory to Amazon consignment stock
  • Records the credit memo correctly
  • Matches Amazon’s customer refund

Sellable and Unsellable FBA Returns

Not every customer return is placed back into sellable inventory.

Amazon may classify returned inventory as:

  • Sellable
  • Customer damaged
  • Carrier damaged
  • Warehouse damaged
  • Defective
  • Unsellable

The integration should use this disposition to determine whether the unit returns to sellable consignment stock or requires an adjustment, reimbursement claim, disposal, or removal order.

Consignment Pick-Up: Returning Unsold Inventory

Slow-moving FBA inventory can generate storage and aged-inventory charges. Merchants may also want to discontinue a product, change packaging, or move stock to another sales channel.

In these situations, the merchant creates a removal order in Seller Central.

When Amazon returns the inventory, SAP processes a consignment pick-up.

The inventory movement is:

Amazon consignment stock under special stock W → merchant-owned warehouse inventory.

This closes the inventory loop for goods sent to Amazon but never sold.

Amazon FBA Inventory Reconciliation in SAP

Inventory reconciliation is one of the most important differences between Amazon FBA and FBM.

Under FBM, the merchant physically controls the inventory. Under FBA, Amazon stores, transfers, fulfills, and adjusts the inventory across its network.

FBA units can become:

  • Lost inside the fulfillment network
  • Damaged by Amazon
  • Damaged by a carrier
  • Disposed of
  • Transferred between fulfillment centers
  • Reserved for customer orders
  • Classified as unsellable
  • Reimbursed by Amazon

SAP consignment stock should be reconciled periodically against Amazon’s FBA inventory ledger and adjustment reports.

Inventory Reconciliation Data

The reconciliation should compare:

  • Opening FBA inventory
  • Inbound shipment receipts
  • Customer shipments
  • Customer returns
  • Fulfillment-center transfers
  • Lost or damaged units
  • Disposals
  • Removal orders
  • Amazon reimbursements
  • Closing FBA inventory

Confirmed differences should create the appropriate SAP inventory or financial adjustment.

Without regular reconciliation, the quantity recorded as Amazon consignment stock in SAP will gradually differ from the quantity Amazon is actually holding.

Amazon Settlement and Payment Reconciliation

Amazon commonly settles merchant proceeds on an approximately 14-day cycle, although timing can vary by marketplace, account status, reserves, and transaction type.

A settlement can include:

  • Product sales
  • Customer refunds
  • Amazon commissions
  • FBA fulfillment fees
  • Storage charges
  • Removal and disposal fees
  • Advertising charges
  • Promotional costs
  • Inventory reimbursements
  • Account reserves
  • Other adjustments

The amount deposited into the bank is therefore not a simple total of customer invoices. It is a net settlement containing several transaction types.

Automated Amazon Settlement Reconciliation

A scalable Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA integration should:

  1. Import Amazon settlement data.
  2. Match sales against SAP consignment issues and invoices.
  3. Match returns against credit memos.
  4. Post fees to the correct general-ledger accounts.
  5. Identify reimbursements and inventory adjustments.
  6. Clear the Amazon customer account.
  7. Highlight unmatched differences for review.

If order-level fees are mapped correctly, most transaction-level amounts can reconcile automatically.

Charges That May Require Separate Postings

Some account-level charges may not be directly linked to individual customer orders:

  • Monthly FBA storage fees
  • Aged-inventory surcharges
  • Advertising expenses
  • Removal and disposal fees
  • Reserve adjustments
  • Other account-level deductions

These exceptions remain manageable when the main sales, return, fee, reimbursement, and settlement flows are automated.

Benefits of Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA Integration

True FBA Inventory Visibility

The business always knows how much inventory is sitting at Amazon. Special stock indicator W keeps this inventory visible, correctly classified, and valued on the balance sheet.

Accurate Revenue Recognition

Revenue is recognised when the customer purchase creates a consignment issue, not when inventory is sent to Amazon in bulk.

Automated Accounts Receivable

Sales, returns, credit memos, fees, and reimbursements can flow into SAP without large volumes of manual journal entries.

Reconcilable Marketplace Tax

Ship-to-driven statistical tax can be compared with Amazon’s Marketplace Facilitator reporting.

Reliable Inventory Accounting

Amazon-held inventory remains visible as a merchant-owned asset without being confused with stock physically available in the merchant’s warehouse.

Faster Financial Close

Automated settlement matching reduces the time finance teams spend investigating payouts, fees, refunds, and unexplained differences.

Exception-Based Operations

Finance and operations teams can focus on genuine exceptions instead of manually processing every Amazon transaction.

Scalability

The same closed-loop integration can support 100 orders per day or 10,000 orders per day without requiring a proportional increase in finance and operations headcount.

Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA Integration Process Summary

The complete process works as a closed loop:

  1. Consignment fill-up: Inventory moves to Amazon without revenue recognition.
  2. Consignment issue: A customer sale consumes FBA inventory and creates revenue and receivables.
  3. Consignment return: Eligible customer returns restore Amazon consignment stock and create credit adjustments.
  4. Consignment pick-up: Unsold inventory returns to the merchant’s warehouse.
  5. Inventory reconciliation: Lost, damaged, disposed, transferred, and reimbursed units are recorded.
  6. Settlement reconciliation: Sales, fees, returns, reimbursements, and reserves are matched to Amazon payouts.

Conclusion

Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA integration is best designed around SAP’s customer consignment cycle.

The consignment fill-up moves stock to Amazon without recognising revenue. The consignment issue records the customer sale. Consignment returns and pick-ups keep inventory and receivables aligned. Inventory and settlement reconciliation connect SAP records with what Amazon is physically holding and financially paying.

Getting the customer master, consignment order types, ASIN mappings, ship-to information, fee conditions, tax logic, and reconciliation rules right creates a scalable foundation for Amazon growth.

The result combines Amazon’s fulfillment network with SAP S/4HANA’s inventory control and financial discipline—without losing visibility over inventory the merchant still owns.

In the next part of this series, we will cover implementation best practices and the most common mistakes businesses make when integrating Amazon FBM and FBA with SAP S/4HANA.

Frequently Asked Questions About Amazon FBA SAP S4 HANA Integration

Here are some questions that are usually asked. 

What is the difference between Amazon FBA and FBM in SAP?

With FBA, inventory is sent to Amazon in bulk and Amazon fulfills customer orders. It is best modelled as a consignment process.

With FBM, the merchant stores and ships each order. It normally follows the standard SAP order-to-cash process.

Why is Amazon FBA treated as consignment in SAP?

Inventory stored at Amazon remains the merchant’s property until a customer buys it. Amazon has physical custody, but ownership has not transferred.

What are the main SAP consignment steps for Amazon FBA?

The four main steps are consignment fill-up, consignment issue, consignment return, and consignment pick-up. Not all of them will be applicable. 

When is revenue recognised for an Amazon FBA sale?

Revenue is recognised when the customer purchases the product and SAP creates the consignment issue. Revenue is not recognised when inventory is initially sent to Amazon.

What is special stock indicator W in SAP?

Special stock indicator W identifies customer consignment stock. It keeps Amazon-held inventory visible as merchant-owned inventory while separating it from normal unrestricted stock.

Does sending inventory to Amazon create an invoice?

No. A consignment fill-up represents an inventory movement, not a customer sale. It should not create revenue or a standard customer invoice.

Who should be the Sold-To Party and Payer?

Amazon typically remains the Sold-To Party and Payer because Amazon settles the merchant’s proceeds. The end customer’s address is used as the Ship-To information.

Why is the customer ship-to address required?

The customer’s delivery address determines the tax jurisdiction. SAP needs this information to calculate and reconcile tax with Amazon’s Marketplace Facilitator reporting.

How should Amazon-collected sales tax be recorded?

It is often recorded as a statistical condition. This keeps the tax visible for reconciliation without treating it as tax the merchant must remit where Amazon is responsible.

How are Amazon FBA customer returns handled in SAP?

A sellable customer return creates a consignment return, return delivery, inventory movement back into special stock W, and a credit memo linked to the original transaction.

What happens when a returned product is unsellable?

The Amazon disposition should determine whether the unit requires an inventory adjustment, reimbursement, disposal, or removal order instead of returning to sellable consignment stock.

What happens to unsold Amazon FBA inventory?

The merchant can create a removal order in Seller Central. SAP then processes a consignment pick-up when Amazon returns the units.

How should lost or damaged FBA inventory be handled?

SAP consignment inventory should be reconciled with Amazon’s FBA inventory reports. Confirmed lost, damaged, disposed, or reimbursed units should create appropriate inventory and financial adjustments.

How often does Amazon pay FBA merchants?

Amazon commonly settles merchant proceeds approximately every 14 days. The timing can vary by marketplace, account, reserve requirements, and transaction type.

Why does the Amazon payout not equal total product sales?

The payout is net of customer refunds, commissions, fulfillment fees, storage charges, advertising costs, reserves, reimbursements, and other adjustments.

Can Amazon settlement reconciliation be fully automated?

Most order-related reconciliation can be automated when sales, returns, fees, and reimbursements are mapped correctly. Some account-level charges may still require separate postings or review.

Can the integration support high Amazon order volumes?

Yes. A scalable integration uses automated document creation, API processing, scheduled reconciliation, and exception management. The same consignment design can support hundreds or thousands of daily orders.

entry.title: Inquiry on Rounding value issue during Partial Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We would like to seek clarification regarding the system rounding logic during Goods Receipt posting, as we observed inconsistent rounding behavior which results in a discrepancy between the Goods Receipt amount and the Invoice amount.

 Observation

Based on the Goods Receipt quantity and PO unit price calculation:

7,025.200 × RM 4.32 = RM 30,348.864

However, the system updates the EKBE-DMBTR = RM 30,348.87

The system rounds the amount to RM 30,348.87.

 

Expected Result

We expected the amount to remain RM 30,348.86

 

Inquiry

Could you please clarify the following:

  1. Why does SAP round up RM 30,348.864 to RM 30,348.87 when updating EKBE-DMBTR?
  2. Which standard program, function module, or rounding routine performs this rounding?
  3. We have observed that in other test scenarios the system does not round the value in the same way. Could you explain under which conditions SAP rounds the amount and under which conditions it does not?

entry.title: SAP BTP Trial (us10-001) - CAP MTA deployment stuck in STAGING, droplet not created
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
 
I am facing a deployment issue on an SAP BTP Trial account (Cloud Foundry region us10-001).
 
Environment
-----------
BTP Trial Account
Cloud Foundry Runtime
Region: us10-001
SAP BAS
@SAP/cds: 9.9.1
@SAP/cds-dk: 9.9.3
MBT: 1.2.34
 
Issue
-----
My CAP application builds successfully and the MTAR is generated without errors.
 
Commands executed:
 
mbt build
 
Output:
- cds build completed successfully
- MTAR created successfully
- mta_archives/mycapapp_1.0.0.mtar generated
 
Deployment
----------
 
cf deploy mta_archives/mycapapp_1.0.0.mtar
 
Deployment progresses to:
 
Creating application "mycapapp-ui"
Creating application "mycapapp-srv"
Creating application "mycapapp-db-deployer"
 
Uploading application "mycapapp-ui"
Uploading application "mycapapp-srv"
Uploading application "mycapapp-db-deployer"
 
Staging application "mycapapp-ui"...
Staging application "mycapapp-srv"...
Staging application "mycapapp-db-deployer"...
 
The deployment then hangs indefinitely.
 
Diagnostics Performed
---------------------
 
1. Reproduced in DEV space.
2. Reproduced in QA space (completely clean space).
3. HDI service created successfully.
4. XSUAA service created successfully.
5. Applications are created successfully.
6. Package upload succeeds.
7. Node.js buildpack assigned successfully.
 
Cloud Foundry API Results
-------------------------
 
cf app mycapapp-db-deployer
 
Shows:
 
requested state: stopped
buildpacks:
stack:
 
Then checking via CF API:
 
cf curl "/v3/apps/<guid>/builds"
 
returns:
 
state: STAGING
droplet: null
 
The build remains in STAGING for a long time and no droplet is ever created.
 
Questions
---------
 
1. Is anyone experiencing similar staging issues on the us10-001 Trial landscape?
2. Is there a known Cloud Foundry Trial platform issue causing builds to remain in STAGING?
3. Are there additional CF diagnostics that can identify why the droplet is never created?
 
Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.
 
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Chart of Depreciation created with Reference and can...
entry.content:
Hi!

Error ACC_AA116 states that there are missing parallel depreciation area for Depreciation Area 34.

In S/4HANA, for each posting in real time area, you must also have a parallel area assigned to Group Currency that adopt values from the posting area.

It is important to mention that the if you plan to use more the same Chart of Depreciation for more than one Company Code, the customizing between must be the same in regards of Depreciation Areas and Currencies. Otherwise, please consider to have different Chart of Depreciation for each Company Code.

In KBA 0003002785 we have an example on how your customizing should look like:

Transaction OADB:
Chart of Depr. XXYY

Depreciation area   1   Ledger group 0L   Accounting principle LGAP    Posting in real time
Depreciation area 31   Ledger group 0L   Accounting principle LGAP    Does not post
Depreciation area 32   Ledger group 2L   Accounting principle IFRS     Posting in real time
Depreciation area 33   Ledger group 2L   Accounting principle IFRS     Does not post
Depreciation area 42   Ledger group 4L   Accounting principle ABCD    Posting in real time
Depreciation area 43   Ledger group 4L   Accounting principle ABCD    Does not post

Transaction OABT:
Chart of Depr. XXYY                      

AreaName of Depreciation AreaCurrency Type ValAdIdAPC Ttr IdntTrm
1 Book deprec. Ledger group 0L / Accounting principle LGAP 0 0 
31Parallel Curr Area for Area 1, Ledger group 0L / Accounting principle LGAP30 Group Currency 1x1x
32Book deprec. Ledger group 2L / Accounting principle IFRS        0 0 
33Parallel Curr Area for Area 32, Ledger group 2L / Accounting principle IFRS       30 Group Currency    32x32x
42Book deprec. Ledger group 4L / Accounting principle ABCD  0 0 
43Parallel Curr Area for Area 42, Ledger group 4L / Accounting principle ABCD30 Group Currency    42x42x

Once customizing is in order, the error should be resolved.

entry.title: Inbound delivery deleted in ERP but available in EWM
entry.content:
The system is S/4 HANA 2021 version.  There is a inbound delivery is created against a PO and distributed to EWM. Then it is been processed in EWM through PRDI or Run inbound process. Then there is a queue error for the inbound delivery after the processing which shows as "Notified quantity exceeds quantity.......". When the PO is checked the purchase order history, the GRN is already reflected for the full quantity. Then to check it in ERP, in VL33n, the delivery is shown as "Archived or deleted". There is another IBD which holds the GRN for the same quantity.

The above is the scenario when the incident is reported as all the issue was against the standard behaviour. The user deleted the IBD which is processed in EWM and where the delivery is stuck in queue, but yet created a new Inbound delivery and processed it, where the stock is reflected.

In ERP stock check, the quantity is doubled i EWM, and ERP stays normal. Even the physical and ERP stock is matched, so only in EWM the stock is inconsistent. Taking this as a reference, tried to recreate a test case in quality system, but the deletion process is cant be done. There are certain questions with the scenarios:

1)What could have been happened in first place, as in which hierarchy the process taken place to achieve the scenario?
Maybe the user could have done the GRN, then without knowing it he tried again the process. Then finds out he is wrong after the processing the delivery, so there he is deleting it, even in table the delivery is deleted.

2)How system allowed to delete a EWM distributed delivery? 
3)Against the delivered quantity in PO, how system allowed to create a IBD? 
4)How it again allowed to distributed in EWM?

Here, the scenario maybe little confusing, but the business demands a fast paced solution. Will be happy to share more details if needed.

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series – Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit in SAP T
entry.content:
Hi @Aleksey_Belskii : 

Could you please advise whether there are any fields available in BOPF that are not exposed in the field catalog 

We are currently looking for the following information:

  1. Number of containers assigned to a Freight Order/Freight Booking

    • Example BOPF field: /SCMTMS/D_TORROT-QUA_PCS_VAL
    • We would like to determine whether this field, or an equivalent field, is available in the field catalog.

  2. Original Order Reference

    • Example BOPF field: /SCMTMS/D_TORITE-BASE_BTD_ID
    • We would like to understand whether the original order reference can be accessed through the field catalog 

Could you please suggest:

  • Whether these fields are available in any standard field catalog.
  • If not, whether there is an alternative field/catalog that can be used to retrieve the same information.
  • Any recommended approach to expose these BOPF attributes for reporting, selection, or document determination purposes.

entry.title: Re: SAC/DSP to connect with SAP ECC (underlying oracle da...
entry.content:
Hi @sumitsamal123 

Yes, it is possible to connect SAP ECC → SAP Datasphere → SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and build real-time or near real-time analytics.

Recommended Approach

  1. Verify SAP ECC Prerequisites

    • Ensure your SAP ECC system meets the minimum DMIS (Data Migration Server) version supported by SAP Datasphere.
    • For example, SAP generally requires a supported DMIS release (such as DMIS 2018 SP level6 or higher depending on the scenario and SAP support matrix).
    • Also ensure that the SLT component is available, either:
      • Embedded within the ECC system, or
      • Installed on a dedicated SLT server (if its not feasible to upgrade the DMIS of ECC, then you upgrade the DMIS of SLT box matching the DMIS requirements for  datasphere connectivity.)

  2. Configure SLT Replication (either slt embedded in ECC or standalone, below steps are same)

    • SLT acts as the data replication framework between ECC and SAP Datasphere.
    • In transaction LTRC, create a Mass Transfer ID (MTID) and configure the source ECC connection, and make sure to select RMS during creation of MTID
    • SLT uses database triggers and logging tables to capture changes for delta replication.

  3. Connect ECC to SAP Datasphere

    • Create the source system connection in Datasphere using the SLT based integration approach.
    • For ECC systems, it is often recommended to perform direct table replication 
    • Data to be ingested to Datasphere

  4. Initial Load and Delta Replication

    • Perform the initial load from ECC to Datasphere.
    • Once completed, SLT automatically captures incremental changes through delta mechanisms.
    • New records and updates are continuously replicated to Datasphere based on the configured replication schedule.

  5. Connect SAP Datasphere to SAP Analytics Cloud

    • Establish a Live Connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere.
    • Build stories, dashboards, and analytical models in SAC on top of Datasphere datasets.
    • As new delta records arrive in Datasphere, the latest data becomes immediately available for reporting and visualization in SAC.

Suggest raising a ticket with SAP to get on the DMIS requirement.

 

Regards,

Bheemesh

entry.title: WPT Is Not Just Configuration — It Is Warehouse Movement Intelligence
entry.content:
Warehouse Process Type Is Not Just Configuration — It Is the Execution Intelligence Behind Every Warehouse Task

Manyat Thinking: Simplicity is the Ultimate Sophistication

Warehouse Process Type Is Not Just Configuration — It Is the Execution Intelligence Behind Every Warehouse Task

Warehouse Process Type Is More Than Configuration: A Different Way to Think About SAP EWM

Introduction

Whenever we learn SAP EWM, Warehouse Process Type (WPT) is usually introduced with a familiar definition:

"A configuration object used to control Warehouse Task creation and execution."

There is nothing technically wrong with this explanation. However, one question always stayed in my mind:

If WPT is only a configuration object, why does almost every warehouse movement depend on it?

That question completely changed the way I think about Warehouse Process Types. Today, I no longer see WPT as just another configuration object. Instead, I see it as the point where a warehouse movement begins to receive its execution behavior.

A Warehouse Task Can Execute… But Can It Decide?

A Warehouse Task tells an operator what to move, where to move it, and how much inventory should be moved.

However, can a Warehouse Task decide whether that movement represents Putaway, Picking, Replenishment, or an Internal Movement?

The answer is no.

That decision already exists before the Warehouse Task is created. The Warehouse Task simply executes the movement, while the Warehouse Process Type determines the business nature of that execution.

That is the difference.

Every Movement Looks Similar Until Business Gets Involved

From a purely technical perspective, every Warehouse Task moves inventory from one place to another.

From a business perspective, however, every movement serves a completely different objective.

A Putaway movement organizes warehouse storage.

A Picking movement fulfills customer demand.

A Replenishment movement protects the picking process.

An Internal Movement improves warehouse operations.

Although the physical movement may appear identical, the business purpose behind each movement is completely different. That business purpose is what changes the expected behavior of the movement, and this is where Warehouse Process Type becomes important.

Think About a Chess Game

The analogy that helped me understand this concept came from chess.

Every chess piece moves across the same board, yet nobody expects a Pawn to behave like a Queen. The board remains the same and the destination may even be identical, but the movement behavior is fundamentally different.

Warehouses operate in a very similar way.

Every Warehouse Task moves inventory, but a Putaway task should never behave like a Picking task. Likewise, a Replenishment task should never behave like an Internal Movement.

The inventory may be identical. The operator may be identical. Even the forklift may be identical.

What changes is the expected behavior of the movement.

That behavior begins with the Warehouse Process Type.

Does WPT Work Alone?

No.

This is one of the biggest misunderstandings people often have when discussing Warehouse Process Types.

I am not suggesting that WPT replaces Storage Type Search, Queue Determination, WOCR, POSC, Wave Management, or any other SAP EWM control object. Every one of these components has its own responsibility within warehouse execution.

However, before SAP EWM can apply all of those controls, the system must first understand one fundamental question:

What kind of warehouse movement is this?

That execution identity originates from the Warehouse Process Type.

The remaining EWM components then refine, optimize, and control the execution of that movement.

Why Should Business Care?

Many warehouse problems are solved by introducing additional configuration.

Sometimes, however, the actual issue is much simpler.

The movement behavior itself was never designed correctly.

A well-designed Warehouse Process Type contributes to cleaner execution, greater operational consistency, easier troubleshooting, improved resource utilization, and lower operational confusion.

The objective is not to create more Warehouse Tasks.

The objective is to create better warehouse behavior.

WPT Also Tells a Story

One interesting observation is that Warehouse Process Types do more than control execution.

They also reveal warehouse behavior.

For example, frequent Emergency Replenishment often indicates weaknesses in inventory planning.

An unusually high number of Internal Movements may suggest poor slotting decisions or warehouse imbalance.

Repeated exception tasks frequently expose unstable business processes.

Warehouse Tasks explain what happened.

Warehouse Process Types often help explain why it keeps happening.

This is where operational visibility begins to generate business value.

Looking Ahead

Warehouses continue to evolve through Robotics, Automation, MFS, and Artificial Intelligence.

All of these technologies are capable of moving inventory.

However, moving inventory is not the difficult part.

Understanding how each movement should behave is.

Automation without well-defined movement behavior simply allows mistakes to happen faster.

Good warehouse execution begins long before a forklift starts moving.

It begins when warehouse movements are designed correctly.

Conclusion

Warehouse Tasks move inventory, but movement alone never creates an efficient warehouse.

Efficient warehouses depend on predictable movement behavior.

Storage Types, Queues, WOCR, POSC, and other SAP EWM components all contribute to warehouse execution. However, before those controls can be applied, Warehouse Process Type establishes the execution identity of the movement.

That is why I no longer see WPT as just another configuration object.

I see it as the point where warehouse movement begins to receive its execution behavior.

Manyat Thinking

Warehouse Tasks execute movement.

Warehouse Process Types define movement behavior.

The goal is not to move inventory faster.

The goal is to ensure every movement behaves correctly before execution begins.

— Rahul Kumar

entry.title: Introducing API Rate Limits for SAP SuccessFactors: What You Need to Know
entry.content:
SAP has announced SAP API Policy and SAP SuccessFactors is introducing API rate limiting to improve stability, reliability, and performance for all customers and partners.

What is changing?

Soft rate limits are being applied to all existing customers. API requests will still be executed, but standardized rate limit headers will be returned in the API response header.

Hard rate limits will be enforced for all new public APIs from H2 2026 (November 13, 2026) and for all new tenants created on January 1, 2027 and after. API requests that exceed a hard rate limit will be rejected with a 429 error response code.

The following soft rate limits will be in effect in 2608 monthly release (August 17, 2026):

API Rate Limit
Tenant Level rate limit: global rate limit for all API requests across the tenant. Applies to all endpoints and operations.600 requests/minute
/odata/v2/$metadata25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/$batch25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/upsert25 requests/minute
/sfapi/v1/soap75 requests/minute
/odata/v2/FormContent25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/FormHeader50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/Goal50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/NominationTarget50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/TalentRatings25 requests/minute; Quota: 1,250/hour
/odata/v2/Candidate50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/CandidateLight50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplication75 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplicationInterview25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplicationOnboardingData25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplicationQuestionResponse25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplicationStatus25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobApplicationStatusAuditTrail25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobOffer25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobOfferApprover25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobReqFwdCandidates25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobReqScreeningQuestion25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobRequisition50 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobRequisitionLocale25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobRequisitionOperator25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/JobRequisitionPosting25 requests/minute
/odata/v2/OfferLetter25 requests/minute

Why are we making these changes?

SAP has introduced a new API Policy to ensure platform stability, reliability, and fair usage across all customers. Adopting this policy, SuccessFactors is implementing standardized rate limits to protect the platform from abusive traffic patterns and ensure consistent performance for all users.

What improvements can be expected?

  • Improved server stability and reliability for all customers.
  • Protection against abusive traffic patterns that degrade performance.
  • Standardized rate limit headers in API response headers will notify customers before hard limits are enforced, giving time to adjust usage.

How will customers be affected?

  • Existing customers: Soft rate limits will be in place, and you will receive the standardized rate limit headers in the API response header in 2608 monthly release. Detailed information will be posted on WNV in 2608 monthly release. No API requests in your integrations will be blocked at this stage. Some APIs already had hard rate limits in place for some tenants, and those hard rate limits will continue to remain in effect. In the future, when we convert all soft rate limits to hard rate limits, we will standardize the thresholds across all rate limits to keep consistence.
  • New customers (from January 1, 2027): Hard rate limits will apply from the start.
  • New APIs (H2 2026 onwards): All new public APIs will have hard rate limits enforced from launch.
  • Customers whose applications exceed rate limits may receive a 429 error once hard limits are enforced.

Next Steps / Customer Action Items

We understand that you may have questions and concerns, and we are here to support you through this transition. Here are the next steps:

  • Action Item 1 – Review your current API usage and reduce your API usage against the rate limit thresholds listed above after August 17, 2026, when soft rate limit headers will appear in API response headers. We will convert all soft rate limits to hard rate limits for all customers in the future and communicate in advance with detailed information and timelines.
  • Action Item 2 – Monitor the standardized rate limit headers in API response headers starting with the 2608 monthly release (August 17, 2026). Detailed information will be posted on WNV.
  • Action Item 3 – Prepare for hard rate limits for any new APIs you are developing (H2 2026) and for new tenants provisioned on or after January 1, 2027.
  • Action Item 4 – Handle 429 errors in your customer applications (e.g., BTP applications) by implementing appropriate retry logic and backoff strategies.

Have A Question?

For questions regarding this feature, please leave a comment below.

Need Support?

If you encounter any issues, please log a case to the relevant component.

entry.title: SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF - Workflow parameter input field disappears when property is assigned
entry.content:
HI @C_SAP_ForumUser,

Thank you for your suggestion. I implemented the solution exactly as recommended using both extension points:

  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF
  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

The custom workflow condition is successfully displayed in Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions.

However, I am facing an issue while trying to provide F4 Value Help for the custom parameter.

What I have tested

1. Without OData configuration

Parameter contains only:

  • id
  • name
  • shorttext
  • xsd_type
  • mandatory
  • ddic_type

Result

  • Input field is displayed.
  • No F4 help is available

2. Using a Custom CDS View (Scenario = Value Help)

I created a custom CDS View based on I_PurchaseRequisitionType and published it successfully.

Configured in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF:

service_path
entity
property

Result

  • The parameter input field disappears completely.

3. Using SAP Standard Workflow Value Help

Instead of the custom CDS, I used the standard workflow service:

  • Service: S_MMPURWORKFLOWVH_CDS
  • EntitySet: C_PurReqnTypeForWorkflow
  • Property: PurchasingDocumentType

I verified from the $metadata that:

  • Service exists
  • EntitySet exists
  • Property exists

However, after assigning the property in the parameter definition, the workflow UI no longer displays the input field.

Summary of my observations

Configuration Result
id + name + mandatory Input field displayed
+ ddic_typeInput field displayed
+ service_pathInput field displayed
+ entity Input field displayed
+ propertyInput field disappears completely

It appears that the Workflow framework rejects the parameter once the property is specified.

My Question

Has anyone successfully implemented an F4 Value Help for a custom workflow condition parameter in SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF?

If yes, could you please share:

  1. Whether a standard or custom OData service was used?
  2. Does the property require additional ValueList/Common.ValueList annotations?
  3. Is F4 Help currently supported for custom workflow condition parameters in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, or is this a framework limitation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Consuming SAP CPI Keystore Entries via Security Content OData API – Part 1
entry.content:
Introduction

I would like to explore how to access and consume Security Content information from SAP Cloud Integration (CI) using the CI Security Content OData API.

In Part 1, I'll configure the required API access, generate OAuth credentials, and test the Security Content API using Postman. "The goal of this first part is simply to establish secure access to the Security Content API. Once the API call works in Postman, the same configuration can be reused inside a CPI integration flow."

In Part 2, I'll build an SAP Cloud Integration iFlow that consumes the API, processes the response, and extracts keystore information for monitoring and reporting scenarios.

These APIs are useful for automating certificate inventory, monitoring certificate expiry, generating reports, or integrating security metadata with external monitoring tools.

Prerequisites: Before getting started, ensure that:

  • You have access to an SAP Integration Suite tenant.
  • You have permissions to create service instances and service keys in SAP BTP.
  • Postman is installed for API testing.

Locate the Security Content OData API

SAP Cloud Integration exposes Security Content information through a dedicated OData API. This API provides access to security artifacts such as:

  • Keystore Entries
  • Certificates
  • Key Pairs
  • User Credentials
  • OAuth Credentials

Navigate to the SAP API Business Hub and search for the Security Content API. Review the available resources and operations to understand the information exposed by the service. You can download the api collection file (OpenAPI JSON) and this can be imported directly into Postman.
URL for API Hub: https://api.sap.com/api/SecurityContent/overview

Create an API Instance

To consume the Security Content API, an API service instance must be available in your SAP BTP subaccount. If an API instance has not already been created:

  • Navigate to your SAP BTP subaccount.
  • Open the service instances section.
  • Create a new instance for the Cloud Integration API service.
  • Complete the instance creation wizard.

This API instance provides secure access to the Cloud Integration APIs using OAuth 2.0 client credentials. And when creating the API instance, ensure the service instance has the required authorization. This role is required to read monitoring and security metadata from the tenant.

Required role:  MonitoringDataRead

Why this matters: Without the proper role, requests to the Security Content API may return authorization errors such as 403 Forbidden.

Create a Service Key

After the API instance is created, generate a Service Key. The service key contains the OAuth credentials required by external clients such as Postman or SAP Cloud Integration to authenticate against the API.

It contains all information needed for OAuth authentication, including:

  • OAuth token URL
  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • API endpoint URL (Base URL for the OData API)

Test the API Using Postman

Import the Postman Collection called Security Content downloaded from Step1.

Add OAuth 2.0 Authentication

Click Get New Access Token, authenticate using the client credentials, and save the generated token at the collection level so that all requests in the collection automatically inherit it.

In the collection settings:

  • Auth Type: OAuth 2.0
  • Grant Type: Client Credentials
  • Access Token URL: Token URL from the service key
  • Client ID: Client ID from the service key
  • Client Secret: Client Secret from the service key

Update Collection Variables

Update the existing baseUrl collection variable with the API endpoint from the service key. This will makes requests reusable across environments and tenants.
baseUrl  https://<ci-api-host>/api/v1

Test the Keystore Entries Request: 

Open Keystore Entries request from the collections.
Authorization: Open the Authorization tab and select-  "inherit auth from parent". This reuses the OAuth token configured at the collection level.
Endpoint: {{baseUrl}}/KeystoreEntries?$select=Alias,Status,Type,ValidNotAfter,KeyType

Execute the Request: 

By default, the API returns an json OData response. you can request Atom/XML by including the appropriate Accept header as "application/atom+xml" If preferred.

Example fields include: Alias, KeyType, ValidNotAfter, Status.

Lessons Learned:

  • Created an independent API instance for accessing SAP CI OData services as needed.
  • Assigned the required MonitoringDataRead permission.
  • Generated a service key, Configured and verified OAuth 2.0 authentication in Postman and can be used in CI as is smoothly.
  • Verified that the API's are accessible using OAuth 2.0 and able to receive the data in XML or Json
What can be better:
  • what I notice is that the OData couldn't able to filter the records using "ValidNotAfter" fields, it could be very useful if it works so that I do not have to use extra functions to filter the data & the same time reducing the amount records into CI Runtime, means smaller payloads.

entry.title: VA14L displays multiple lines for each order
entry.content:
There seems to have been a change in the layout of transaction VA14L, which makes the use of the transaction a lot less clear than before. VA14L now displays multiple lines for each order.
Where before you would see just 1 line for an order.

What has changed here and why? And is it possible to reverse this change?  We would like to see just 1 line for an order. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP for Me システム登録情報の主要ソフトウェア製品変更
entry.content:
SAP for Meに登録済みのシステム情報について、主要ソフトウェア製品（Primary Software Product）の変更方法をご教示いただけますでしょうか。

## 対象システム
SID：*
システム番号: CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER86
インストール番号：DIGITS

## 背景
当該システムは、もともとSAP ERP 6.0として運用しておりましたが、
その後SAP S/4HANA DIGITSへコンバージョンしております。
上記製品変更に加え、基盤クラウド・ハードウェアの変更もあったためSAP for Me上で既存エントリとは別にシステム新規登録しましたが、
登録時の設定誤りにより、主要ソフトウェア製品を従来の「SAP ERP 6.0」のまま登録してしまいました。

現在、実際のシステムはSAP S/4HANA DIGITSとして稼働しておりますが、SAP for Me上の登録情報のみ「SAP ERP 6.0」となっている状況です。

## 確認した内容
SAP for Me上のシステム編集画面から変更を試みましたが、以下の制約により変更できませんでした。
  - 現在の登録が「SAP ERP 6.0」の場合、変更先として「SAP ERP 6.0」または「SAP R/3」のみ選択可能
    「SAP S/4HANA」関連製品は選択肢として表示されない
  - 「SAP S/4HANA」として登録済みのシステムについては、S/4HANAの各バージョンへの変更が可能

## 変更希望内容
現在：
  Primary Software Product = SAP ERP 6.0
変更後：
  Primary Software Product = SAP S/4HANA DIGITS

## 質問事項
SAP for Meに登録済みのシステムについて、上記の通り変更する方法についてご教示いただけますでしょうか。
また、ユーザー側で変更できない仕様の場合は、以下について併せてご教示いただければ幸いです。
  - SAPにて登録情報の修正対応が可能か
  - システム再登録等の対応が必要か
  - 必要な申請手順や提供情報


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me システム登録情報の主要ソフトウェア製品変更
